sections.heading: Product Description
sections.content:
Product Name: Gel-Cream Eye Cream
Format: Gel-cream emulsion
Primary Container: 15mL airless pump bottle
Batch Sizes:
• Laboratory scale: 5 kg
• Pilot scale: 50 kg
• Commercial scale: 500 kg

sections.heading: Key Active Ingredients
sections.content:
The following heat-sensitive actives must be handled with care throughout the manufacturing process:
• Caffeine
• Vitamin K
• Peptides (specific peptide complex to be confirmed)

All three actives are classified as heat-sensitive and must not be subjected to elevated temperatures during addition or processing. Maximum addition temperature: [To be confirmed — recommend ≤40°C as a starting guideline, pending stability data].

sections.heading: Process Flow Summary
sections.content:
Step 1 — Phase Preparation: Prepare oil and water phases separately in designated vessels. Heat as required for emulsification base (excluding heat-sensitive actives).

Step 2 — High-Shear Homogenization: Combine oil and water phases and subject to high-shear homogenization to form the gel-cream emulsion. Homogenization speed, duration, and temperature profile: [To be confirmed during lab-scale trials].

Step 3 — Cool-Down with Overhead Mixing: Reduce batch temperature using cooling jacket or heat exchanger. Maintain gentle overhead mixing throughout cool-down to preserve emulsion integrity and ensure homogeneity.

Step 4 — Addition of Heat-Sensitive Actives: Once batch has cooled to the confirmed safe addition temperature, introduce caffeine, Vitamin K, and peptides sequentially or as a pre-blend. Continue overhead mixing post-addition until fully incorporated.

Step 5 — Final Checks Pre-Fill: Conduct in-process quality checks (pH, viscosity, appearance) before releasing batch to filling line.

Step 6 — Filling: Fill at room temperature into 15mL airless pump bottles at a target line speed of 40 units per minute.

sections.heading: Fill Operation Details
sections.content:
Fill Temperature: Room temperature (approximately 20–25°C)
Container: 15mL airless pump bottle
Target Line Speed: 40 units per minute
Filling Equipment Type: [To be confirmed — recommend airless-compatible piston or peristaltic filler]
Fill Volume Accuracy Target: [To be confirmed — recommend ±0.5% as starting tolerance]
Target Annual Output (Year 1): 120,000 units

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: This document outlines the equipment requirements for manufacturing the Gel-Cream Eye Cream across laboratory, pilot, and commercial batch scales. Equipment specifications should be validated at each scale prior to formal production.

sections.heading: Mixing and Emulsification Equipment
sections.content:
High-Shear Homogenizer
• Required at: Lab (5 kg), Pilot (50 kg), and Commercial (500 kg) scales
• Purpose: Emulsification of oil and water phases into gel-cream base
• Capacity Requirements:
  - Lab: ≥5 kg capacity vessel
  - Pilot: ≥50 kg capacity vessel
  - Commercial: ≥500 kg capacity vessel
• Key Specifications: Variable speed control, CIP/SIP compatibility, temperature monitoring capability, stainless steel contact surfaces (316L recommended)
• Rotor-stator configuration: [To be confirmed during process development]
• Speed range required: [To be confirmed]

Overhead Mixer
• Purpose: Gentle agitation during cool-down phase and post-active addition
• Must be capable of operation at low shear to protect heat-sensitive actives (caffeine, Vitamin K, peptides)
• Variable speed with torque monitoring: Recommended
• Anchor or paddle impeller: [To be confirmed based on viscosity profile]
• Must be compatible with same vessels as homogenizer, or transfer to dedicated cool-down vessel required: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Temperature Control Equipment
sections.content:
• Jacketed vessels with heating and cooling capability required for emulsification and cool-down stages
• Cooling rate control is critical — rapid cooling may cause emulsion instability; controlled cool-down profile to be established during lab trials
• In-line or immersed temperature probes (calibrated) required at all scales
• Target active addition temperature: [To be confirmed — ≤40°C recommended as starting point]
• Room temperature filling environment: 20–25°C; HVAC control in filling area required

sections.heading: Filling Line Equipment
sections.content:
Filler Type: Airless-compatible filling machine
• Recommended type: Piston filler or peristaltic pump filler with airless bottle compatibility
• Target Line Speed: 40 units per minute
• Fill Volume: 15 mL
• Fill Accuracy Target: ±0.5% (to be confirmed)
• Must handle gel-cream viscosity without shear degradation
• In-line checkweighing: Recommended for fill weight verification
• Container handling: Designed for airless pump bottle format (upright orientation, pump-down filling or bottom-up as appropriate for airless mechanism)

Capping / Pump Assembly Equipment
• Airless pump insertion and torquing station: [To be confirmed — manual or automated]
• Torque specification: [To be confirmed]

Labeling Equipment
• [To be confirmed — label format, applicator type]

Annual Volume Requirement (Year 1): 120,000 units
• At 40 units/minute and an assumed [To be confirmed] operating hours per day, estimated production days required: [To be confirmed based on OEE targets]

sections.heading: Cleaning and Sanitization Equipment
sections.content:
• CIP (Clean-In-Place) system compatibility required for all product-contact equipment at pilot and commercial scales
• Lab-scale: Manual cleaning acceptable with validated cleaning procedure
• Cleaning validation requirements: [To be confirmed with QA — particularly important given peptide actives which may require specific analytical methods for residue testing]
• Cleaning agents and concentrations: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Calibration Requirements
sections.content:
The following instruments must be on a calibrated schedule:
• Temperature probes / thermocouples
• Homogenizer speed controllers (tachometers)
• Overhead mixer speed controllers
• Filling machine dosing system
• In-line checkweigher
• pH meters (for in-process testing)
• Viscometers (for in-process testing)
• Balance / scales (all sizes used in dispensing and fill weight checks)

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.content: This document defines the in-process quality control checks to be performed during manufacturing of the Gel-Cream Eye Cream (15mL airless pump bottle). These checks are designed to detect deviations early and protect the integrity of heat-sensitive actives including caffeine, Vitamin K, and peptides.

sections.heading: Critical Process Parameters (CPPs)
sections.content:
The following parameters have been identified as critical based on the manufacturing process:

• Homogenization speed and duration — affects emulsion droplet size and stability
• Temperature at active addition — must not exceed safe threshold to protect caffeine, Vitamin K, and peptides
• Cool-down rate — affects emulsion structure and active integrity
• Overhead mixing speed post-active addition — must be low-shear to avoid peptide degradation
• Fill temperature — room temperature (20–25°C) must be maintained
• Fill weight / fill volume accuracy — directly impacts product dose

sections.heading: In-Process Test Schedule
sections.content:
PHASE PREPARATION
• Test: Visual appearance of each phase (clarity, color, absence of undissolved material)
• Frequency: Each batch, each phase
• Acceptance Criteria: [To be confirmed]
• Operator action if OOS: Investigate undissolved materials before proceeding

PRE-ACTIVE ADDITION (COOL-DOWN)
• Test: Batch temperature
• Frequency: Continuous monitoring; record at time of active addition
• Acceptance Criteria: ≤[To be confirmed]°C
• Operator action if OOS: Continue cooling; do not add actives above threshold

POST-HOMOGENIZATION (PRE-ACTIVE ADDITION)
• Test: pH
• Frequency: Once per batch
• Acceptance Criteria: [To be confirmed]
• Test: Viscosity
• Frequency: Once per batch
• Acceptance Criteria: [To be confirmed] cPs
• Test: Visual appearance (homogeneity, color, phase separation)
• Frequency: Once per batch
• Acceptance Criteria: Uniform, smooth gel-cream, no visible phase separation

POST-ACTIVE ADDITION
• Test: pH
• Frequency: Once per batch
• Acceptance Criteria: [To be confirmed — delta vs pre-addition acceptable range TBC]
• Test: Viscosity
• Frequency: Once per batch
• Acceptance Criteria: [To be confirmed]
• Test: Appearance
• Frequency: Once per batch
• Acceptance Criteria: Uniform, no particulates, consistent with standard

FILLING LINE
• Test: Fill weight check
• Frequency: At line start, every [To be confirmed] units, at line stop, and after any interruption
• Acceptance Criteria: [To be confirmed] g ± 0.5% (target tolerance TBC)
• Test: Pump functionality check (actuation, dispensing, no leakage)
• Frequency: 100% visual or [To be confirmed] AQL sampling
• Acceptance Criteria: Full actuation, no leakage, clean dispense
• Test: Closure / pump torque
• Frequency: [To be confirmed]
• Acceptance Criteria: [To be confirmed] Nm
• Test: Container appearance (no damage, correct label orientation)
• Frequency: [To be confirmed] AQL level
• Acceptance Criteria: No visible defects per approved standard

sections.heading: Out-of-Specification (OOS) Procedure Summary
sections.content:
• Any OOS result must be immediately reported to the Batch Supervisor.
• Production should be placed on hold pending investigation.
• Root cause investigation must be documented.
• Disposition of affected batch or batch segment to be determined by QA.
• All OOS events must be recorded in the BMR and linked to a deviation report.
• Formal OOS SOP reference: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Retain Samples
sections.content:
• Retain samples must be taken at the end of every batch.
• Retain quantity: [To be confirmed — recommend minimum 2x full application quantity for stability reference]
• Storage conditions: [To be confirmed — recommend ambient controlled storage matching product shelf-life conditions]
• Retain period: [To be confirmed — typically product shelf life + 1 year for cosmetics; verify with RA]

sections.heading: Purpose and Scope
sections.content: This plan outlines the approach for scaling the Gel-Cream Eye Cream from laboratory (5 kg) through pilot (50 kg) to commercial (500 kg) manufacturing. It defines key scale-up considerations, transfer milestones, and risk areas specific to this formulation and process.

sections.heading: Scale-Up Stages
sections.content:
Stage 1 — Laboratory Scale (5 kg)
• Objective: Establish proof of concept, define process parameters, confirm active addition temperature windows
• Key deliverables: Preliminary formula, homogenization parameters, cool-down profile, active addition sequence, initial in-process specifications
• Acceptance criteria: Meets target appearance, pH, viscosity; actives incorporated without visible degradation

Stage 2 — Pilot Scale (50 kg)
• Objective: Confirm process scalability at 10× scale; validate equipment parameters; generate stability samples
• Key deliverables: Pilot BMR, scale-up process parameters, formal IPQC results, packaging compatibility data with 15mL airless pump bottle
• Key risks at this stage:
  - Heat distribution in larger vessel may alter cool-down rate — monitor closely
  - High-shear homogenizer energy input per unit mass may differ — re-optimize speed/time
  - Peptide shear sensitivity should be re-assessed at larger overhead mixer scale
• Acceptance criteria: Meets full IPQC specification; yield ≥[To be confirmed]%

Stage 3 — Commercial Scale (500 kg)
• Objective: Confirm reproducibility at full commercial scale; qualify filling line at 40 units/minute; validate cleaning procedures
• Key deliverables: Commercial BMR, process validation protocol and report (3 consecutive batches recommended), filling line qualification, cleaning validation
• Acceptance criteria: Three consecutive batches meeting full specification; fill weight Cpk ≥[To be confirmed]; yield ≥[To be confirmed]%

sections.heading: Critical Scale-Up Considerations
sections.content:
Heat-Sensitive Actives Management
• Caffeine, Vitamin K, and peptides must be added only after confirmed cool-down at all scales
• As vessel size increases, cool-down time will increase — cooling jacket efficiency and surface-area-to-volume ratio must be assessed
• A documented, validated temperature profile for cool-down must be established at each scale

High-Shear Homogenization
• Specific energy input (kJ/kg) should be maintained as close as possible across scales, not simply replicating RPM or time
• Emulsion droplet size analysis (e.g., laser diffraction) is recommended as a scale-up marker
• Homogenization parameters at commercial scale: [To be confirmed during pilot]

Overhead Mixing During Cool-Down
• Impeller geometry and tip speed should be assessed at each scale to ensure consistent, low-shear mixing
• Peptides in particular are susceptible to mechanical shear — mixing speed should be the minimum required to maintain homogeneity

Filling Line Qualification
• Airless pump bottle filling at 40 units/minute requires line qualification with actual product at commercial viscosity
• Fill weight accuracy (Cpk) must be demonstrated across line start, mid-run, and end of batch
• Line speed may need adjustment based on actual gel-cream flow characteristics: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Technology Transfer Documentation Requirements
sections.content:
The following documents must be completed and approved prior to each scale transition:
• Updated Batch Manufacturing Record (BMR) for new scale
• Equipment qualification records (IQ/OQ/PQ) for any new equipment
• Analytical method transfer confirmation (where applicable)
• Risk assessment for the scale transition
• Training records for operators at the receiving site or scale
• Change control record: [To be confirmed — if moving between sites or manufacturing units]

sections.content:

Color / Finish: [To be confirmed]
Supplier: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Compatibility Considerations
sections.content:
Airless System and Gel-Cream Viscosity
• Airless pump systems are viscosity-sensitive — the gel-cream must be within the pump's operable viscosity range
• Target viscosity at room temperature (fill and use condition): [To be confirmed]
• Pump manufacturer's recommended viscosity range: [To be confirmed from supplier]
• If viscosity is outside pump range, formulation or pump selection adjustment will be required

Active Ingredient Compatibility with Container
• Caffeine: Generally compatible with PP/PE packaging — [Confirm with supplier]
• Vitamin K: Light-sensitive; consider whether container provides adequate UV protection (opaque or UV-blocking packaging recommended): [To be confirmed]
• Peptides: Confirm no interaction with inner piston or bag material — adsorption to container surfaces is a known risk with some peptides: [To be confirmed via compatibility study]

Fill at Room Temperature
• No hot-fill concerns for the container
• Room temperature fill is compatible with standard airless pump bottle specifications

sections.heading: Packaging Compatibility Testing Requirements
sections.content:
The following compatibility and performance tests should be conducted prior to commercial launch:

• Extractables and leachables study: [To be confirmed — assess migration from container/pump materials into the gel-cream formulation]
• Pump actuation performance: Dose weight per actuation, actuation force, number of actuations to prime, total actuations delivered
• Product recovery rate: % of product deliverable from airless container (target typically ≥90%): [To be confirmed]
• Stability testing in primary container: Minimum 12-month real-time + accelerated (40°C/75% RH): [To be confirmed with RA/QA]
• Drop and transport testing: [To be confirmed]
• Seal / closure integrity testing: [To be confirmed]
